payload.query: During the 1990s and early 2000s, Alan Abramowitz and Mo Fiorina debated the extent and nature of partisan polarization among the mass public. What was each argument and evidence? How has the literature evolved since? Which argument is best supported by recent scholarship?

articles.title: Did This Artist’s Career Bloom Because Her Mother’s Career Died?
articles.abstract: Andrea Fraser had long felt that she was to blame for the years her mother, Carmen de Monteflores, was overlooked. Now Carmen is 92. Can the Whitney Biennial make amends?

articles.title: South Texas Democrats Will Steer Party’s Direction, Left or Center

articles.title: Shigeko Sasamori, Hiroshima Survivor Who Preached Peace, Dies at 92
